headline: Malaysia's Fintech Sector Raises RM3.6B in 2025 Funding
context: Malaysian fintech companies raised RM3.6 billion in venture capital and growth funding during 2025, led by payments and lending platforms, though 68% of funding went to just 5 companies headquartered in Kuala Lumpur.

cards.big: RM3.6 billion raised by Malaysian fintechs — but 5 KL companies captured 68% leaving 280 startups fighting for scraps
cards.sub: Funding concentration mirrors Malaysia's wider economic centralisation in Klang Valley

cards.big: Malaysian fintech revenue hit RM8.2 billion but profitability remains elusive — sector lost RM1.1 billion collectively
cards.sub: Customer acquisition costs averaging RM85 per user mean most fintechs burn through capital before reaching sustainability

cards.big: P2P lending platforms disbursed RM4.8 billion to 82,000 SMEs — filling gaps left by bank loan rejections
cards.sub: Default rates averaging 5.8% on P2P platforms create investor risk that conventional banking regulation doesn't cover

cards.big: SC's fintech regulatory sandbox graduated only 12 of 38 participants — 26 failed to meet viability criteria
cards.sub: Sandbox approach means consumers are testing unproven products with real money in lightly regulated environments

cards.big: Celebrating fintech funding while the sector collectively loses RM1.1 billion is confusing investment with value creation
Should SC require fintech companies to achieve profitability within 5 years of licensing or face sandbox exit?
